repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

With GCC 15.2.0 from `nix-shell`, `CC=gcc`, `CI_BUILD=ON`,
`CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug`, and `-Werror`, building
`src/nvim/CMakeFiles/nvim_bin.dir/channel.c.o` fails with two warnings promoted
to errors:

```text
/tmp/neovim/channel-info-stream-warning-before/src/nvim/channel.c: In function ‘channel_close’:
/tmp/neovim/channel-info-stream-warning-before/src/nvim/channel.c:176:9: error: ignoring return value of ‘freopen’ declared with attribute ‘warn_unused_result’ [-Werror=unused-result]
  176 |         freopen("/dev/null", "w", stderr);
      |         ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
In file included from /tmp/neovim/channel-info-stream-warning-before/src/nvim/channel.c:9:
/tmp/neovim/channel-info-stream-warning-before/src/nvim/channel.c: In function ‘channel_info’:
/tmp/neovim/channel-info-stream-warning-before/src/nvim/api/private/helpers.h:33:35: error: ‘stream_desc’ may be used uninitialized [-Werror=maybe-uninitialized]
   33 | #define CSTR_AS_OBJ(s) STRING_OBJ(cstr_as_string(s))
      |                                   ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
/tmp/neovim/channel-info-stream-warning-before/src/nvim/channel.c:1044:25: note: in expansion of macro ‘CSTR_AS_OBJ’
 1044 |   PUT_C(info, "stream", CSTR_AS_OBJ(stream_desc));
      |                         ^~~~~~~~~~~
/tmp/neovim/channel-info-stream-warning-before/src/nvim/channel.c:1005:15: note: ‘stream_desc’ was declared here
 1005 |   const char *stream_desc, *mode_desc;
      |               ^~~~~~~~~~~
cc1: all warnings being treated as errors
```

## Solution

Name the `freopen()` result and return the channel stream descriptor from the same switch that adds stream-specific metadata.

comment_data.body:
Wouldn't it be enough to just add `default: UNREACHABLE;` to the existing switch statement.

Splitting the function like this just to trick gcc for one variable is extremely myopic.
Ah. I just figured that if future enums are added then we'd miss the "missing enum" warnings in the future. I've pushed the fix.

> I just figured that if future enums are added then we'd miss the "missing enum" warnings in the future.

I think that's the exact reason why there isn't a default branch.
Perhaps make the `default:` branch GCC-only? So that clang can still gives the `-Wswitch` warning.
Looking at some  similar code where I think we have had the same problem, it might actually be enough to just put `UNREACHABLE;` _after_ the switch statement (with no default branch, or extra init)
except that one would need to also change each `break;` to an appropriate goto. bleh. I'd experiment a bit if could just reproduce it which I can't:p

comment_regular_data.body: It's a bit strange I don't get the same warning, I use GNU gcc 15.2.1 but I doubt one patch release completely changes it. would you mind post the entire configure and build command lines?

With the power of nix, I reproduced these behaviors. Note that this behavior is basically identical on the other prs I made (and that I will make sure to not spam in the way I did in the future... :P):

Warning:
- Nix GCC 13.4.0, `CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug`, `CI_BUILD=ON`
- Nix GCC 14.3.0, `CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug`, `CI_BUILD=ON`
- Nix GCC 15.2.0, `CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug`, `CI_BUILD=ON`

No Warning:
- Nix GCC 15.2.0, Release, CI_BUILD=ON
- Nix GCC 15.2.0, RelWithDebInfo, CI_BUILD=ON
- Ubuntu 24.04 GCC 13.3.0
- Fedora 42 GCC 15.2.1
- Fedora 43 GCC 15.2.1
- openSUSE Tumbleweed GCC 15.2.1
- Fedora/Arch GCC 16.1.1
